title: Venidium plantagineum
taxonomy: Venidium plantagineum is classified within the family Boraginaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as borage or forget-me-nots. The species Venidium plantagineum belongs to the genus Venidium, which is a small group of plants. Further classification places it within the order Boraginales and the clade asterids. Overall, the classification of Venidium plantagineum reflects its relationships with other flowering plants within the asterid clade.

constituents: Venidium plantagineum, also known as the "fire cracker plant" due to its ability to cause severe pain and blistering upon contact with the skin. The main active constituents of Venidium plantagineum are venidium alkaloids, specifically venidium, which are known to cause intense pain and irritation upon contact. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
